text:
BOTTOM LINE — Small caps outperformed amid risk rebound post-Q1 correction, energy lagged on oil drop despite Middle East tensions easing, positioning shows protection demand with elevated PCR. Housing led baskets as vol spikes signal accumulation in cyclicals. Traders watch small cap momentum vs gamma flips.

REGIME — Risk-on, small caps led, energy lagged, housing surged.

INDICES — VIX 17.5 down vs 20d MA 23.4. IWM +2.16%, DIA +1.81%, QQQ +1.31%, SPY +1.21%. Small > large by 0.85pp. SLV +3.35% most notable cross-asset.

SECTORS — XLY +2.36%, XLI +1.87%, XLK +1.53%, XLRE +1.53%, XLV +1.49%, XLP +1.26%, XLF +0.77%, XLB +0.25%, XLC +0.23%, XLU -0.41%, XLE -2.76%. WGMI +5.35%, JETS +4.84% tops; XOP -4.84%, USO -7.79% bottoms.

THE 7s — Housing 7 +3.50% (DHI strongest, strongest DHI; weakest unknown), Old Metals 7 +2.84%, Industrial 7 +2.67%, Buildout 7 +2.58%, Fab 7 +2.36%. Energy 7 -2.80% (weakest unknown, strongest unknown), New Metals 7 -0.91%, Power 7 -0.78%, Defense 7 -0.30%, New Staples 7 -0.22%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29, CME hold 100%. Stance: Hawk 6 (Cook,Hammack,Kashkari,Logan,Barkin,Goolsbee), Neutral 5 (Powell,Williams,Barr,Jefferson,Paulson), Dove 4 (Bowman,Miran,Waller,Daly).

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.07 elevated protection, skew +7.4% put premium fear; spot 689 near maxpain 689 GEX_flip 699. QQQ PCR_vol 2.54 high fear, IWM PCR_vol 1.55. VIX 17.5 below 20d MA 23.4 complacency.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil USO -7.79% dragged XLE -2.76% Energy 7 -2.80% as Strait of Hormuz eased. Silver SLV +3.35% decoupled from gold GLD +1.33% on risk rebound.

STYLE & FACTOR — Value BULL zscore +1.08 days 385 dominant.

VOLUME — Elevated DIA 1.6x IWM 1.1x accumulation on gains; XLE 1.6x XLY 1.0x on mixed price. Declining IJH 0.5x XLC 0.5x ignored.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 62.1 Greed. Top movers NFLX -9.72% New Staples, WGMI +5.35% Industries, JETS +4.84% Industries. 20d momentum XLK +11.64% best sector, Semi 7 +29.45% best basket; XLU -0.12% worst sector, Energy 7 -5.04% worst. Vol spikes NFLX 3.2x -9.72% distribution, EIDO 2.8x +1.97% accumulation.

WATCH — IWM +2.16% small outperformance vol 1.1x; NFLX -9.72% vol spike 3.2x distribution; DHI Housing 7 lead post-earnings April 21.
